medications.indication_rationale: Regular combined analgesia controls sinus pain; source advises ibuprofen 400mg + paracetamol 1000mg every 6 hours.
medications.dose_route_frequency: ibuprofen 400mg + paracetamol 1000mg orally every 6 hours (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Short-term decongestion of the osteomeatal complex to aid drainage; STRICT max 3 days to avoid rebound. Source used xylometazoline 0.1% max 3 days.
medications.dose_route_frequency: per product directions, MAXIMUM 3 days (as per source note)
contraindications_in_this_case: Do not exceed 3 days — rhinitis medicamentosa

medications.indication_rationale: Mechanical clearance of secretions relieves pressure; no rebound risk. Use boiled-cooled or sterile water only.
Not indicated at day 5 without bacterial criteria (>10 days/double-sickening/severe). Purulent discharge alone does not justify antibiotics.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do saline irrigation after a hot shower; use only boiled-cooled or sterile water; keep head upright, avoid bending.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Steam loosens secretions; sterile water prevents introducing pathogens; upright posture reduces sinus pressure.

patient_education_points.point: This is a viral sinus infection following your cold. Antibiotics don't help in the first ~10 days; the priority is opening the nose and washing out mucus with saline rinses. It usually settles over 10–14 days.
Only use the medicated decongestant spray for up to 3 days, and use only boiled-cooled or sterile water for sinus rinses.
Go to ED / call 000 for swelling or redness around the eyes, double vision, a severe unrelenting headache, neck stiffness or confusion.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Whether bacterial criteria develop (persistence >10 days, double-sickening); complications.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Eye swelling/double vision, severe headache, neck stiffness or confusion — emergency; or symptom worsening after initial improvement — GP.

must_include_items: Symptomatic care: regular analgesia, saline irrigation, short (<=3 day) decongestant
Orbital/intracranial safety-net (eye swelling/diplopia, severe headache, neck stiffness, confusion)
acceptable_alternatives: Analgesia choice flexible; intranasal corticosteroid is an acceptable adjunct
GP review at day 10 or sooner if worsening
errors_of_commission: Prescribing antibiotics at day 5 for purulent discharge
Sending an uncomplicated sinusitis with a negative complication screen to ED by ambulance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires withholding antibiotics, symptomatic care, and an orbital/intracranial safety-net.
